subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear explanation and application of the key concept of change
Frequent, organization-specific links to Health-1 throughout the analysis
Revisits the concept in multiple analytical sections (7Ps, NPM, regression, conclusion)
weaknesses: Integration sometimes feels compartmentalized with generic textbook statements
Abrupt transitions into definitions without deeper framework discussion
Some paragraphs describe marketing elements without explicitly connecting back to change theory
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work solidly in Band 4: the student shows strong understanding and links change to Health-1âs marketing revamp at multiple points. However, integration is not fully pervasiveâgeneric definitions and abrupt transitions prevent a Band 5. A score of 4 reflects strong but not complete integration.

strengths: Provides four supporting documents (within the 3â5 requirement)
Documents are generally relevant (financial summaries, rebranding visuals, screenshots)
In-text referencing is consistent and enhances evidence linkage
weaknesses: Depth of some documents is uneven (e.g., limited numeric detail in map listing)
Citation formatting is inconsistent (footnotes vs. superscripts)
Range of ideas moderate rather than extensive
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets the quantity requirement (Band 4) but document relevance and depth fall in Band 3âdocuments are sufficiently in-depth but do not offer wide-ranging new insights. A score of 3 balances quantity and moderate depth.

strengths: Uses appropriate tools (7 Ps, net-profit margin, regression) relevant to the research question
Each tool is applied to Health-1âs data, illustrating link to profitability
weaknesses: Selection of tools is adequate but not varied or especially effective
Applications are surface-level (no regression equation, missing RÂ² discussion, limited critique)
Relevance is clear but analytical rigor is inconsistent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments indicate an âadequateâ selection and application (Band 3), but the superficial treatment of regression and limited theoretical critique warrant a modest reduction. A score of 2 reflects basic but underdeveloped use of tools.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Sufficient selection and use of monthly financial data with transparent NPM tables
Analysis effectively links trends to the research question
Integration of qualitative discussion with quantitative evidence is mostly effective
weaknesses: Evaluation of alternative factors and data limitations is brief
Time-lag effects, seasonality, and anomalies are acknowledged but not fully explored
No metrics on online engagement included
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student achieves Band 4 for sufficient data use and mostly effective analysis. A fully sustained, thorough integration (Band 5) is not met due to brevity in evaluating limitations and external factors, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

strengths: Conclusions fully consistent with the presented evidence
Explicitly answers the research question, linking marketing revamp to increased profitability
weaknesses: Lacks critical reflection on study limitations
Restates findings rather than deepening insight or suggesting future considerations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the conclusion fits Band 3 for consistency and direct answer to the RQ, the absence of evaluative depth or reflection prevents full marks. A score of 2 reflects accurate but unreflective conclusions.

strengths: Logical organization with a clear table of contents and headed sections
Tables and figures are captioned clearly
weaknesses: Some TOC section titles do not match in-text headings
Transitions between sections could be smoother for readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure is appropriate overall (Band 2) but minor inconsistencies in headings and section transitions suggest a score of 1 to reflect a need for tighter alignment.

strengths: All required IB elements are present (title page, word count, bibliography, appendices)
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in citation style (footnotes vs. hyperlinks)
Visual formatting (e.g., image metadata) could be improved for clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets basic requirements (Band 2) but small formatting inconsistencies and missing metadata justify a score of 1 rather than full marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure with table of contents, headed sections and appendices is maintained throughout, satisfying the descriptor for âappropriate structureâ (Band 2).

**Strengths:** Financial summaries, rebranding visuals, website screenshots and map listing are all relevant and generally detailed.  
**Limitations:** Depth is unevenâe.g., supporting document 4 offers minimal numeric insight and some images add little fresh evidence. Overall relevance is solid but the range of ideas is moderate, fitting Band 3 (relevant and sufficiently in-depth).
All IB-required elementsâtitle page with RQ and word count, contents page, bibliography, and labelled appendicesâare present; therefore the top band for presentation is deserved.
Four distinct supporting documents are provided and clearly referenced in-text. This quantity is within the 3â5 requirement and maximises the available mark under this strand.

**Strengths:** Each tool is applied to Health-1 data and linked to profitability; the regression chart illustrates the ad-spend relationship.  
**Limitations:** Applications remain surface-level (no regression equation, no discussion of causality vs correlation, limited critique of 7 Ps). Relevance is clear but analytical rigour is mixed, so Band 3 is most accurate.

A wide set of monthly financial data is extracted and tabulated; figures are used correctly in NPM calculations and regression. Selection is sufficient and purposeful, matching Band 4 (âsufficient selection and useâ).

**Strengths:** Analysis links trends in NPM and advertising spend to the RQ, and considers how rebranding resolved earlier weaknesses. Integration of qualitative discussion with quantitative evidence is mostly effective.  
**Limitations:** Evaluation of alternative factors, time-lag effects and limitations of data is brief, preventing a âthorough, sustainedâ treatment. Hence Band 4 is appropriate.
The student directly answers the RQâasserting that the revamped marketing increased profitabilityâthus fulfilling the highest descriptor for this strand.

The student employs the 7 Ps, net-profit-margin ratio and simple linear regressionâan adequate suite of tools for the RQ, though not especially varied. Selection is therefore âadequateâ rather than âlimitedâ or âhighly effectiveâ, corresponding to Band 3.
The conclusion restates financial findings and logically follows from the presented evidence; no contradictions are detected, meeting the descriptor for full consistency (Band 3).

**Strengths:** All analysis is firmly grounded in Health-1. Detailed proprietary monthly accounts, photographs, website screenshots and map listings demonstrate frequent, organisation-specific linkage. Throughout the narrative the student continuously returns to how each marketing activity affected Health-1âs results.  
**Limitations:** Some generic textbook statements (e.g., definition of branding, description of 7 Ps) momentarily weaken integration.  
Overall the criterion shows a strong, largely integrated organisational connection, matching Band 4.

**Strengths:** The student explains the key concept of change, links it to the need for a marketing revamp, and revisits the concept in several analytical sections (7 Ps, NPM, regression, conclusion).  
**Limitations:** Discussion of change is still compartmentalised; some paragraphs talk generally about branding or digital presence without explicitly tying back to the wider implications of organisational change. The analysis is therefore only partially, not fully, integrated across the whole IA.  
Given the clear but not completely pervasive integration, the work aligns best with Band 4 (strong understanding with partial integration).

annotations.comment: Consider explicitly linking the concept of change to Health-1âs competitive environment rather than general branding comments.
The table of contents is well organized, improving navigation through clear structure.
Some section titles in the TOC do not precisely match in-text headings; ensure consistency for reader ease.
Introduction effectively uses Figure 1 to illustrate outdated branding, grounding the problem in real visuals.
The transition from context to definition of change is abrupt; provide a deeper discussion of organisational change frameworks.
Good concrete examples of Health-1âs rebranding steps link back to organisational change needs.
The generic branding definition interrupts flow; focus on applying marketing theory explicitly to Health-1âs logo update.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Include critique of one branding element (e.g., QR code card) using a business model to deepen analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The title page includes all required IB elements (RQ, word count, personal code), enhancing presentation clarity.
The definition of the key concept of change ends abruptly and lacks depth; elaborate on how change theory informs strategic marketing.
Conclusions restate findings but lack critical reflection; incorporate evaluation of limitations to deepen insight.
Strong integration of supporting document references for website and map listing enhances relevance.
No metrics on online engagement are provided; include clickthrough or map view data to substantiate impact.
Add quantitative data from Supporting Document 3 (website analytics) to demonstrate change in client reach.
The 7 Ps analysis contextualises each element well for Health-1âs strategy.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Acknowledging limitation of 7 Ps is good but lacks linkage to data; quantify how processes improvement affected retention.
Link each P back to net profit margin trends to demonstrate relevance to research question.
Selection of net profit margin is appropriate and aligns clearly with assessing profitability.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Regression application remains superficial; include the fitted equation and discuss $$R^2$$ to clarify strength of correlation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Data table effectively presents monthly NPM calculations with transparent formulas.
The narrative attributes all change to marketing; discuss time lags and external factors (seasonality, competitor actions).
Regression table omits correlation statistics; add coefficient of determination to quantify strength of relationship.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Caption and table layout are clear, aiding reader comprehension of regression input.
Numerous negative gross profit line items warrant commentary; highlight anomalies in analysis.
Discuss Decemberâs prepaid expenses spike and its effect on margin stability to demonstrate evaluation rigor.
Supporting Document 4 map listing visually demonstrates online presence, adding depth.
The conclusion logically follows presented evidence and addresses the research question directly.
Bibliography entries are consistently formatted, supporting academic integrity.
Footnotes citation style is inconsistent; standardize superscript formatting and hyperlink to full references.
Supporting Document 1 provides comprehensive financial detail; great depth of raw data.
The map image lacks capture date metadata; include timestamp or filter settings to fully substantiate findings.
Advertisement disclaimer text is truncated; add missing contraindications to improve professional accuracy.
